avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche danger is assessed as considerable (level 3) from 1800 metres. It will remain very mild during the night and spontaneous avalanches are still possible in the early morning from avalanche paths that have not yet been discharged. The avalanches can tear through to the ground in the northern sector (documented avalanches on Wednesday impressively demonstrated the situation). In the touring area, releases are possible in very steep gullies and bowls on the shady slopes with little additional load.
Medium to large gliding avalanches can slide from grass mats at any time.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Be careful on very steep slopes in the touring area - persistent weak layer problem!

snowpackStructure.comment: The warming has already allowed the snowpack to settle a little. Due to the mild temperatures and solar radiation, the snowpack is rapidly losing its firmness. On the northern slopes, the kinetic metamorphism (cup crystals in the snow layer close to the ground!) continues to weaken the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Continuation of high pressure weather with a frost line at an altitude of 2700 metres. Daytime changes in avalanche danger are expected.

weatherForecast.comment: On the mountains, the night will be clear and mostly frost-free. The north-westerly wind will weaken. Thursday will continue with clear weather. The sun will shine, but it will be slightly clouded by high cloud fields. In the morning, high fog may accumulate below an altitude of 1000 metres in the peripheral mountains.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is moderate at higher altitudes. Wet loose snow avalanches can come loose, especially in extremely steep terrain. Spontaneous avalanche activity increases during the day with incoming radiation and warming.
Drift snow packs are prone to triggering in high, on shady slopes and can occasionally be triggered as a snow slab in steep slope zones.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Pay particular attention to wet snow problems and isolated persistent weak layers

snowpackStructure.comment: The new and drifting snow cover has already settled somewhat and lost some of its proneness to triggering. In shady high altitudes, the old snowpack with crusts and embedded angular crystals is unfavourably structured and weakened in places. Depending on the aspect, the snow becomes damp or wet during the day changes up to high altitudes, leading to a decreasing firmness and destabilisation of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: With calm, high-pressure weather, the avalanche risk will slowly decrease towards the end of the week.

weatherForecast.comment: With the influence of high pressure, the high-altitude current will slowly turn to the south-west. Thursday night will often be clear and frost-free at high altitudes. On Thursday, sunny weather will prevail most of the time. However, this will not be completely undisturbed, as a few veil clouds will pass over the sky. The temperature at 2000 metres will be between +3 and +6 degrees, at 1000 metres between +5 and +10 degrees. The north-westerly wind will be moderate on the mountains and strong in places during the daytime changes.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is rated as low. Occasional small wet slides are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack is wet or soaked.

tendency.highlights: Continuation of high pressure weather with a frost line at an altitude of 2700 metres.

weatherForecast.comment: On the mountains, the night will be clear and mostly frost-free. The north-westerly wind will weaken. Thursday will continue with clear weather. The sun will shine, but it will be slightly clouded by high cloud fields. In the morning, high fog may accumulate below an altitude of 1000 metres in the peripheral mountains.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Spontaneous wet snow avalanches are still possible!
